api/spec/packages/aip.purpose: Root TypeSpec package for the v3 (AIP-style) OpenMeter API. Compiles domain operation interfaces into api/v3/openapi.yaml (and the Konnect variant) via two root namespace files, then normalises the emitted YAML with post-processing scripts and enforces naming/structural constraints with a custom linter. Primary constraint: all route binding, tag metadata, and security schemes live in src/; domain model and operation definitions live in domain sub-folders that are barrel-imported here.

patterns.name: Route and tag binding at root only
patterns.description: @route and @tag are declared exclusively in src/openmeter.tsp and src/konnect.tsp, never inside a domain sub-folder's operations.tsp. Domain interfaces are mounted by extending them in a root interface body.

patterns.name: Two-step compile pipeline
patterns.description: pnpm generate runs tsp compile, then scripts/flatten-allof.mjs and scripts/seal-object-schemas.mjs post-process the emitted YAML. Never hand-edit the output; always regenerate via make gen-api then make generate.
patterns.example: package.json generate: tsp compile --config tspconfig.yaml ./src && node ./scripts/flatten-allof.mjs ... && node ./scripts/seal-object-schemas.mjs ...

patterns.name: Domain ops imported via index.tsp barrels
patterns.description: Each domain sub-folder exposes an index.tsp barrel; root files import only the barrel, never individual operation files. Importing a domain both directly and via its barrel causes duplicate symbol errors.

patterns.name: Two-namespace compilation (OpenMeter vs Konnect)
patterns.description: main.tsp imports both openmeter.tsp and konnect.tsp to emit two distinct OpenAPI outputs from one shared domain library; the variants differ in security schemes and some sub-route overrides.

patterns.name: Security via @useRef to external YAML
patterns.description: Security schemes are TypeSpec model stubs with @useRef pointing to common/definitions/*.yaml fragments, never inline credential definitions.

patterns.name: Custom linter via createRule, registered in lib/index.js
patterns.description: Every lint rule in lib/rules/ uses the createRule factory and must be registered in the lib/index.js $linter rules array. Error severity blocks gen-api; warning surfaces guidance only. An unregistered rule file has zero runtime effect.
patterns.example: lib/index.js: export const $linter = defineLinter({ rules: [casingRule, friendlyNameRule, ...] })

patterns.name: Sealed, reachable-only emitted schemas
patterns.description: tspconfig.yaml must keep omit-unreachable-types: true and seal-object-schemas: true; seal-object-schemas.mjs further rewrites additionalProperties:{not:{}} to false to preserve closed-schema semantics.
patterns.example: tspconfig.yaml options['@typespec/openapi3']: omit-unreachable-types: true; seal-object-schemas: true

key_file_guides.role: Compilation entry point importing openmeter.tsp and konnect.tsp to produce two OpenAPI variants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A missing import here silently drops a whole namespace from every generated artifact.

key_file_guides.role: Binds all domain interfaces to @route paths and @tag metadata for the self-hosted OpenMeter variant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a domain import without a matching @tagMetadata declaration silently omits the tag description from generated OpenAPI.

key_file_guides.role: Konnect variant root; overrides security schemes and some sub-routes relative to openmeter.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Inline security scheme bodies duplicate config — use @useRef to common/definitions/security.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Post-processes emitted YAML to move sibling properties into allOf branches for SDK generators; idempotent via the x-flatten-allOf marker.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing the x-flatten-allOf marker check causes infinite re-run loops; changing YAML_OPTIONS.lineWidth from 0 creates spurious line-wrap diffs.

key_file_guides.role: Rewrites additionalProperties:{not:{}} to false to keep object schemas closed after compilation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing it lets SDK generators emit overly permissive types.

key_file_guides.role: Registers all linter rules via defineLinter/$linter, consumed by the compiler during gen-api.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new rule file is inert until its export is added to the rules array here — no compile error signals the omission.

key_file_guides.role: Centralised casing helpers and the pascalCaseExceptions acronym list shared by all rules.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding accepted acronyms inline in individual rule files instead of here causes divergence from the canonical set.

key_file_guides.role: Emitter config: output dir, omit-unreachable-types, seal-object-schemas, linter extends @openmeter/api-spec-aip/all.
key_file_guides.watch_for: omit-unreachable-types and seal-object-schemas must stay true; emitter-output-dir must match Makefile/CI expectations.

anti_patterns: Declaring @route or @tag inside a domain sub-folder's operations.tsp — routing is bound only in src/openmeter.tsp and src/konnect.tsp
Hand-editing api/v3/openapi.yaml or api/v3/api.gen.go — always regenerate via make gen-api then make generate
Defining a lint rule without registering it in lib/index.js — the rule compiles but has zero runtime effect
Adding accepted PascalCase acronyms anywhere other than pascalCaseExceptions in lib/rules/utils.js
Importing a domain twice (directly and via its index.tsp barrel) — causes duplicate-symbol compilation errors

decisions.decision: Two root namespace files (openmeter.tsp, konnect.tsp) instead of one parameterised file
decisions.rationale: The variants differ in security schemes and some sub-route overrides, and TypeSpec has no preprocessor; separate files keep the variants explicit rather than buried in emitter workarounds.

decisions.decision: Post-processing scripts run after tsp compile rather than as emitter plugins
decisions.rationale: The emitter produces allOf-with-siblings schemas and additionalProperties:{not:{}} that confuse SDK generators; these are output-level fixups that cannot be done inside the compiler without patching upstream.

decisions.decision: Error-vs-warning severity split in the linter
decisions.rationale: Only structurally breaking rules (friendlyName for SDK-visible names, composition-over-inheritance) block gen-api; stylistic rules (casing, docs, field-prefix) warn so they guide without blocking developer flow.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new domain resource to the v3 spec

api/spec/packages/legacy.purpose: Root TypeSpec package for the v1/v2 OpenMeter REST API and the OpenMeter Cloud API. Compiles to api/openapi.yaml, api/openapi.cloud.yaml, and the Python SDK via make gen-api. Primary constraint: src/ is the composition root holding shared primitives (types.tsp, errors.tsp, filter.tsp, query.tsp) and two emit entry points — src/cloud/main.tsp for cloud OpenAPI and src/main.tsp for the Python SDK — that must stay in sync; new v1 domain features belong here, v3 changes go to api/spec/packages/aip.

patterns.name: Dual entry points kept in sync
patterns.description: src/cloud/main.tsp imports every sub-domain to produce api/openapi.cloud.yaml; src/main.tsp is the separate client-emit entry for the Python SDK. A new sub-domain must be registered in BOTH or it silently vanishes from one output.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every named declaration
patterns.description: Every model, enum, union, and interface must carry @friendlyName, which controls the OpenAPI schema name and the SDK class name. The linter enforces this at error severity — a missing @friendlyName blocks gen-api.

patterns.name: @visibility lifecycle decorators on fields
patterns.description: Use Lifecycle.Read / Lifecycle.Create / Lifecycle.Update to derive request and response shapes from a single model instead of defining separate request/response types.

patterns.name: REST resource templates for CRUD bodies
patterns.description: Request body models derive from TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceCreateModel<T> (POST → {Name}Create), ResourceReplaceModel<T> (PUT → {Name}Update), or ResourceCreateOrUpdateModel<T> (PATCH → {Name}Patch). No ad-hoc RequestBody/Input/Payload types.

patterns.name: Shared primitives centralised in types.tsp
patterns.description: ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource, ResourceTimestamps, CadencedResource, and pagination models live in src/types.tsp under namespace OpenMeter. Sub-domain files reference them (OpenMeter.ULID); never re-declare.
patterns.example: // Sub-domain file uses OpenMeter.ULID — not a local re-declaration

patterns.name: @extension("x-omitempty", true) on filter operator fields
patterns.description: Every operator property in filter models carries @extension("x-omitempty", true) so absent filters are excluded from serialised query strings rather than emitted as null.

patterns.name: @operationId on every interface operation
patterns.description: Explicit camelCase verb+noun @operationId prevents non-deterministic generated SDK function names across Go, JS, and Python.

key_file_guides.role: Cloud-variant compilation entry; imports all sub-domains to produce api/openapi.cloud.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new sub-domain file not imported here is silently excluded from api/openapi.cloud.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Client-emit entry for Python SDK generation via tspconfig.client.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Separate from cloud/main.tsp — new domains must be added to both to keep OpenAPI and Python SDK aligned.

key_file_guides.role: Single source of truth for shared primitives: ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource, pagination models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Re-declaring these in sub-domain files creates divergent $ref targets in generated OpenAPI.

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeterError and error union types carrying the @error decorator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Extending Error without @error breaks OpenAPI error schema generation — error responses are not tagged correctly.

key_file_guides.role: Generic filter operator models; every field needs @extension("x-omitempty", true).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing x-omitempty serialises empty filter fields as null, breaking filter semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Distinct pagination shapes: QueryPagination, QueryLimitOffset, QueryCursorPagination.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mixing pagination shapes across endpoints produces inconsistent generated list signatures.

key_file_guides.role: Registers all v1 linter rules via defineLinter/$linter export.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new rule file has zero effect without registration here; no compile error signals the omission.

key_file_guides.role: Two emitter configs: OpenAPI (cloud) vs Python SDK output dirs; both extend @openmeter/api-spec-legacy/all.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing emitter-output-dir breaks make gen-api path expectations and the CI dirty-tree check; dropping the linter extends disables linting for that emit run.

anti_patterns: Defining new domain models directly in root-level .tsp files instead of a sub-folder with its own main.tsp
Re-declaring primitive types (ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource) in sub-domain files — they live in src/types.tsp
Using `extends Error` without the @error decorator — breaks OpenAPI error schema generation
Omitting @operationId on interface operations — yields non-deterministic generated SDK function names
Adding a sub-domain file without registering it in both src/cloud/main.tsp and src/main.tsp imports

decisions.decision: Two tspconfig files (tspconfig.yaml for OpenAPI, tspconfig.client.yaml for Python SDK)
decisions.rationale: The OpenAPI and Python SDK emitters need different output directories and emitter-specific options that a single config cannot parameterise without complex per-run overrides.

decisions.decision: Shared primitives centralised in types.tsp with Resource base models
decisions.rationale: Keeps schema naming deterministic across sub-domains and prevents duplicate $ref targets that confuse SDK generators and produce mismatched type names.

decisions.decision: friendlyNameRule is error-severity; all other rules are warnings
decisions.rationale: @friendlyName controls SDK class names across Go, JS, and Python, so a missing or wrong name is an irreversible public API breakage that justifies a hard compile block.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 resource with visibility-controlled CRUD
